title: microula trichocarpa
intro: Microula trichocarpa, also known as the Tibetan bugloss or Chinese bugloss, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of China and the Himalayas, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and tall, erect stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Microula trichocarpa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and then used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
